{ "nbformat": 4, "nbformat_minor": 0, "metadata": { "colab": { "provenance": [] }, "kernelspec": { "name": "python3", "display_name": "Python 3" }, "language_info": { "name": "python" } }, "cells": [ { "cell_type": "markdown", "source": [ "# **Predicting Salary and Satisfaction in the AI/Data Workforce**\n", "**Fall 2026 Data Science Project**\n", "\n", "Names: Brandon Wagstaff, Sean Lee, Rebecca Holcomb" ], "metadata": { "id": "UVvVWLuwWWwc" } }, { "cell_type": "markdown", "source": [ "Contributions:\n", "\n", "| Member | Sections | 1-2 Sentence Summary |\n", "|------------------|----------|----------------------|\n", "| Brandon Wagstaff | A, B, C | Came up with initial idea and sourced the dataset. Handled raw data ingestion/cleaning and EDA/Summary Statistics. |\n", "| Sean Lee | D, E | Built/Designed ML algorithm. Trained ML algorithm and did test data analysis. |\n", "| Rebecca Holcomb | F, G, H | Completed final visualizations, conclusion section, and textual descriptions. Contributed to visualizations for the EDA portion. |" ], "metadata": { "id": "Gss3JMBhooQa" } }, { "cell_type": "markdown", "source": [ "# **Introduction**" ], "metadata": { "id": "D0zE8yaBcr2T" } }, { "cell_type": "markdown", "source": [ "The main purpose of this project is to take you through the full pipeline of data science. To do this we will be looking into a dataset titled \"Global AI & Data Jobs Salary Dataset.\" As the title suggests, this dataset focuses on the job market for data and AI roles. As a group of students all either majoring in Computer Science or minoring in Data Science, this is incredibly relevant to us. In this tutorial, we hope to gain important insight for students like ourselves soon to enter the work force in the data and AI fields. By trying to predict salary based on job, personal, and company factors, we can help students direct their efforts now to give themselves the best shot at a higher salary or better understand the salary range they can expect. By building this model we can gain many essential insights, such as what factors are most influential to salary.\n", "\n", "In this tutorial we will be going through the Data Science Lifecycle as follows:\n", "1) Dataset Curation and Preprocessing\n", "2) Summary Statistics and Data Exploration\n", "3) ML Algorithm Design/Development\n", "4) ML Algorithm Training and Test Data Analysis\n", "5) Visualizations\n", "6) Conclusion" ], "metadata": { "id": "TfAfEwcUVuzz" } }, { "cell_type": "markdown", "source": [ "# **Data Curation and Preprocessing**" ], "metadata": { "id": "E284ZNX-zaP1" } }, { "cell_type": "markdown", "source": [ "First, we need to select a dataset to work with. As mentioned in the introductions, we will be using a dataset titled \"Global AI & Data Jobs Salary Dataset\". This dataset provides the exact information we need to gain insights into what factors influence salary in the data and AI job field. \n", "\n", "Rakesh Kolipaka, Ranjith Kumar Digutla, and Uday Kiran Neelam. (2026). Global AI & Data Jobs Salary Dataset [Data set]. Kaggle. https://doi.org/10.34740/KAGGLE/DSV/14943248\n", "\n", "By visiting the link above you will be directed to the Kaggle page (web library containing many datasets) for the dataset. If you don't have a Kaggle account, you will need to create one. Otherwise, log into your account. Next, click the download button in the top right. Select the zip option and download it. Next, locate the zip file on your device and extract it. This can be commonly be done by double clicking the zip file. Now you should have a file in the .csv format named \"global_ai_jobs.csv\". This is what we want. We can then upload this csv to your MyDrive folder in Google Drive. For the sake of this project the subfolder we will create to house the data in MyDrive will be called \"DATA\". Upload the \"global_ai_jobs.csv\" file to the DATA folder. Now that we have the data and folder set up, we are ready to load the data. \n", "\n", "It is important to note the we will be using Python language and Jupyter Notebook for this tutorial. If not already familiar, these would be good things to brush up on before moving forward.\n", "\n", "Python language documentation: https://docs.python.org/3/tutorial/index.html\n", "\n", "Jupyter Notebook documentation: https://docs.jupyter.org/en/latest/\n" ], "metadata": { "id": "tXvpT5trVn-L" } }, { "cell_type": "markdown", "source": [ "**Load the Data:**" ], "metadata": { "id": "rbVUbGPfW2Vs" } }, { "cell_type": "code", "source": [ "#Connecting to drive\n", "\n", "from google.colab import drive\n", "drive.mount('/content/drive')\n", "\n", "import pandas as pd" ], "metadata": pd.read_csv(\"/content/drive/MyDrive/DATA/global_ai_jobs.csv\")\n", "\n", "df.head()" ], "metadata": { "id": "BixLCqlLDUMB", "colab": { "base_uri": "https://localhost:8080/", "height": 394 }, 1 UAE Machine Learning Engineer Reinforcement Learning \n", "1 2 USA AI Engineer LLM \n", "2 3 Brazil Research Scientist Analytics \n", "3 4 India Software Engineer AI Computer Vision \n", "4 5 " ], "metadata": { "id": "nMv3bT8Z8rS_" } }, { "cell_type": "code", "source": [ "#find data types\n", "\n", "df.dtypes" ], "metadata": { "colab": { "base_uri": "https://localhost:8080/", "height": 1000 }, "id": "eVOGX5KaJcVg", "outputId": "608ed5a7-28d7-403f-d7e1-e860b9309121" }, "execution_count": 3, "outputs": [ { "output_type": "execute_result", "data": { "text/plain": [ "id int64\n", "country object\n", "job_role object\n", "ai_specialization object\n", "experience_level object\n", "experience_years int64\n", "salary_usd int64\n", "bonus_usd int64\n", "education_required object\n", "industry object\n", "company_size object\n", "interview_rounds int64\n", "year int64\n", "work_mode object\n", "weekly_hours float64\n", "company_rating float64\n", "job_openings int64\n", "hiring_difficulty_score float64\n", "layoff_risk float64\n", "ai_adoption_score int64\n", "company_funding_billion float64\n", "economic_index float64\n", "ai_maturity_years int64\n", "offer_acceptance_rate float64\n", "tax_rate_percent float64\n", "vacation_days int64\n", "skill_demand_score int64\n", "automation_risk int64\n", "job_security_score int64\n", "career_growth_score int64\n", "work_life_balance_score int64\n", "promotion_speed int64\n", "salary_percentile int64\n", "cost_of_living_index float64\n", "employee_satisfaction int64\n", "dtype: object" ], "text/html": [ "

" ] }, "metadata": {}, "execution_count": 3 } ] }, { "cell_type": "markdown", "source": [ "\"Object\" is a catch all term for some data types so we will manually map those columns to the data type that most makes sense just to make sure. In this case, all 8 will be mapped to string data types." ], "metadata": { "id": "pm6Ry75UEyNG" } }, { "cell_type": "code", "source": [ "#mapping to strings\n", "\n", "df = df.astype({'country': str, 'job_role': str, 'ai_specialization': str, 'experience_level': str, 'education_required': str, 'industry': str, 'company_size': str, 'work_mode': str, })" ], "metadata": { "id": "Dzvct72yMcul" }, "execution_count": 4, "outputs": [] }, { "cell_type": "markdown", "source": [ "Now that we know all those columns are strings, we want to standardize the text. This will help us find duplicates later." ], "metadata": { "id": "Pu-ncwh9FJDj" } }, { "cell_type": "code", "source": [ "#standardizing each str column so that extra white space is removed and all text is lowercase\n", "\n", "df['country'] = (df['country'].str.strip().str.lower().str.replace(r'\\s+', ' ', regex=True)) # last part one or more spaces with a single space\n", "df['job_role'] = (df['job_role'].str.strip().str.lower().str.replace(r'\\s+', ' ', regex=True))\n", "df['ai_specialization'] = (df['ai_specialization'].str.strip().str.lower().str.replace(r'\\s+', ' ', regex=True))\n", "df['experience_level'] = (df['experience_level'].str.strip().str.lower().str.replace(r'\\s+', ' ', regex=True))\n", "df['education_required'] = (df['education_required'].str.strip().str.lower().str.replace(r'\\s+', ' ', regex=True))\n", "df['industry'] = (df['industry'].str.strip().str.lower().str.replace(r'\\s+', ' ', regex=True))\n", "df['company_size'] = (df['company_size'].str.strip().str.lower().str.replace(r'\\s+', ' ', regex=True))\n", "df['work_mode'] = (df['work_mode'].str.strip().str.lower().str.replace(r'\\s+', ' ', regex=True))" ], "metadata": { "id": "S37Hhlr8GNii" }, "execution_count": 5, "outputs": [] }, { "cell_type": "markdown", "source": [ "Now, we need to check for missing values in the dataset. In other words, we want to make sure there are no empty cells." ], "metadata": { "id": "F96lfOMq9bFR" } }, { "cell_type": "code", "source": [ "#checking for NA values\n", "\n", "df.isna().sum()" ], "metadata": { "colab": { "base_uri": "https://localhost:8080/", "height": 1000 }, "id": "Aw3_whLtF7qk", "outputId": "6a7291a0-08a7-4a7e-ddd7-a805fdabbc61" }, "execution_count": 6, "outputs": [ { "output_type": "execute_result", "data": { "text/plain": [ "id 0\n", "country 0\n", "job_role 0\n", "ai_specialization 0\n", "experience_level 0\n", "experience_years 0\n", "salary_usd 0\n", "bonus_usd 0\n", "education_required 0\n", "industry 0\n", "company_size 0\n", "interview_rounds 0\n", "year 0\n", "work_mode 0\n", "weekly_hours 0\n", "company_rating 0\n", "job_openings 0\n", "hiring_difficulty_score 0\n", "layoff_risk 0\n", "ai_adoption_score 0\n", "company_funding_billion 0\n", "economic_index 0\n", "ai_maturity_years 0\n", "offer_acceptance_rate 0\n", "tax_rate_percent 0\n", "vacation_days 0\n", "skill_demand_score 0\n", "automation_risk 0\n", "job_security_score 0\n", "career_growth_score 0\n", "work_life_balance_score 0\n", "promotion_speed 0\n", "salary_percentile 0\n", "cost_of_living_index 0\n", "employee_satisfaction 0\n", "dtype: int64" ], "text/html": [ "
\n", "\n", "\n", " \n", " \n", " \n", " \n", " \n", " \n", " \n", " \n", " \n", " \n", " \n", " \n", " \n", " \n", " \n", " \n", " \n", " \n", " \n", " \n", " \n", " \n", " \n", " \n", " \n", " \n", " \n", " \n", " \n", " \n", " \n", " \n", " \n", " \n", " \n", " \n", " \n", " \n", " \n", " \n", " \n", " \n", " \n", " \n", " \n", " \n", " \n", " \n", " \n", " \n", " \n", " \n", " \n", " \n", " \n", " \n", " \n", " \n", " \n", " \n", " \n", " \n", " \n", " \n", " \n", " \n", " \n", " \n", " \n", " \n", " \n", " \n", " \n", " \n", " \n", " \n", " \n", " \n", " \n", " \n", " \n", " \n", " \n", " \n", " \n", " \n", " \n", " \n", " \n", " \n", " \n", " \n", " \n", " \n", " \n", " \n", " \n", " \n", " \n", " \n", " \n", " \n", " \n", " \n", " \n", " \n", " \n", " \n", " \n", " \n", " \n", " \n", " \n", " \n", " \n", " \n", " \n", " \n", " \n", " \n", " \n", " \n", " \n", " \n", " \n", " \n", " \n", " \n", " \n", " \n", " \n", " \n", " \n", " \n", " \n", " \n", " \n", " \n", " \n", " \n", " \n", " \n", " \n", " \n", " \n", " \n", " \n", " \n", "

If there were NA values, we would run the following code:" ], "metadata": { "id": "odzzAo6wBmJ8" } }, { "cell_type": "code", "source": [ "#Remove rows with NA values from dataframe\n", "\n", "df = df.dropna()" ], "metadata": { "id": "7RF7iGQgEQ3P" }, "execution_count": 7, "outputs": [] }, { "cell_type": "markdown", "source": [ "Now, we will check for duplicate observations." ], "metadata": { "id": "hjNNfX0pE9Bl" } }, { "cell_type": "code", "source": [ "#find duplicates\n", "\n", "df.duplicated().sum()\n" ], "metadata": { "id": "AF-OZIYzGBrL", "colab": { "base_uri": "https://localhost:8080/" }, "outputId": "7131683d-f185-4844-bc41-82b6461efd4e" }, "execution_count": 8, "outputs": [ { "output_type": "execute_result", "data": { "text/plain": [ "np.int64(0)" ] }, "metadata": {}, "execution_count": 8 } ] }, { "cell_type": "markdown", "source": [ "This is also good news! This means there are no duplicated rows we would have to remove. If there were duplicated rows we would run the following code:" ], "metadata": { "id": "OWesJkDk4p5l" } }, { "cell_type": "code", "source": [ "#Remove duplicate rows, keep first occurrences\n", "\n", "df = df.drop_duplicates()" ], "metadata": { "id": "EC2FDyQo4_uK" }, "execution_count": 9, "outputs": [] }, { "cell_type": "markdown", "source": [ "Now we have successfully preped the data for further use! Let's take a quick look at what the dataset looks like now." ], "metadata": { "id": "FVtDFX2pJ3hA" } }, { "cell_type": "code", "source": [ "df.head()" ], "metadata": { "colab": { "base_uri": "https://localhost:8080/", "height": 394 }, "id": "j_I0D1CtKDjU", "outputId": "f4cca9a4-1b4c-49d3-ffa3-f14f3f466543" }, "execution_count": 10, "outputs": [ { "output_type": "execute_result", "data": { "text/plain": [ " id country job_role ai_specialization \\\n", "0 1 uae machine learning engineer reinforcement learning \n", "1 2 usa ai engineer llm \n", "2 3 brazil research scientist analytics \n", "3 4 india software engineer ai computer vision \n", "4 5 germany machine learning engineer computer vision \n", "\n", " experience_level experience_years salary_usd bonus_usd \\\n", "0 entry 0 66465 5395 \n", "1 entry 1 75507 11713 \n", "2 entry 0 41660 5268 \n", "3 senior 6 43268 7975 \n", "4 entry 0 69119 4758 \n", "\n", " education_required industry ... vacation_days skill_demand_score \\\n", "0 master automotive ... 27 12 \n", "1 bootcamp retail ... 27 54 \n", "2 phd healthcare ... 13 12 \n", "3 diploma tech ... 30 80 \n", "4 master retail ... 24 82 \n", "\n", " automation_risk job_security_score career_growth_score \\\n", "0 76 57 65 \n", "1 29 69 60 \n", "2 49 70 59 \n", "3 47 79 65 \n", "4 47 64 52 \n", "\n", " work_life_balance_score promotion_speed salary_percentile \\\n", "0 73 15 55 \n", "1 51 15 58 \n", "2 68 37 13 \n", "3 55 46 74 \n", "4 69 17 21 \n", "\n", " cost_of_living_index employee_satisfaction \n", "0 1.23 76 \n", "1 0.87 67 \n", "2 2.13 61 \n", "3 1.49 56 \n", "4 0.87 72 \n", "\n", "[5 rows x 35 columns]" ], "text/html": [ "\n", "

We will understand the distribution of the variables, identify potential relationships between features, and determine which variables may be useful for later machine learning models." ], "metadata": { "id": "4EjxDn2uFChK" } }, { "cell_type": "markdown", "source": [ "## **Summary Statistics**" ], "metadata": { "id": "hEEYsEpZN6G0" } }, { "cell_type": "markdown", "source": [ "First, we will look at a key summary statistics for all numerical variables in our dataset." ], "metadata": { "id": "g7-Oq0CDYuQW" } }, { "cell_type": "code", "source": [ "df.info()" ], "metadata": { "colab": { "base_uri": "https://localhost:8080/" }, "id": "UxbwqN0gK3xd", "outputId": "1614e032-8037-4e0c-cf71-4f490e084439" }, "execution_count": 11, "outputs": [ { "output_type": "stream", "name": "stdout", "text": [ "\n", "RangeIndex: 90000 entries, 0 to 89999\n", "Data columns (total 35 columns):\n", " # Column Non-Null Count Dtype \n", "--- ------ -------------- ----- \n", " 0 id 90000 non-null int64 \n", " 1 country 90000 non-null object \n", " 2 job_role 90000 non-null object \n", " 3 ai_specialization 90000 non-null object \n", " 4 experience_level 90000 non-null object \n", " 5 experience_years 90000 non-null int64 \n", " 6 salary_usd 90000 non-null int64 \n", " 7 bonus_usd 90000 non-null int64 \n", " 8 education_required 90000 non-null object \n", " 9 industry 90000 non-null object \n", " 10 company_size 90000 non-null object \n", " 11 interview_rounds 90000 non-null int64 \n", " 12 year 90000 non-null int64 \n", " 13 work_mode 90000 non-null object \n", " 14 weekly_hours 90000 non-null float64\n", " 15 company_rating 90000 non-null float64\n", " 16 job_openings 90000 non-null int64 \n", " 17 hiring_difficulty_score 90000 non-null float64\n", " 18 layoff_risk 90000 non-null float64\n", " 19 ai_adoption_score 90000 non-null int64 \n", " 20 company_funding_billion 90000 non-null float64\n", " 21 economic_index 90000 non-null float64\n", " 22 ai_maturity_years 90000 non-null int64 \n", " 23 offer_acceptance_rate 90000 non-null float64\n", " 24 tax_rate_percent 90000 non-null float64\n", " 25 vacation_days 90000 non-null int64 \n", " 26 skill_demand_score 90000 non-null int64 \n", " 27 automation_risk 90000 non-null int64 \n", " 28 job_security_score 90000 non-null int64 \n", " 29 career_growth_score 90000 non-null int64 \n", " 30 work_life_balance_score 90000 non-null int64 \n", " 31 promotion_speed 90000 non-null int64 \n", " 32 salary_percentile 90000 non-null int64 \n", " 33 cost_of_living_index 90000 non-null float64\n", " 34 employee_satisfaction 90000 non-null int64 \n", "dtypes: float64(9), int64(18), object(8)\n", "memory usage: 24.0+ MB\n" ] } ] }, { "cell_type": "markdown", "source": [ "We can see that the dataset contains 90,000 observations and 35 features. This indictaes the sample size is large enough for our analysis. Further, all columns contain complete data with no missing values. This improves reliability for machine learning tasks later on. The dataset also includes a balance of categorical and numerical variables, allowing us to examine qualitative characteristics and quantitative measures related to the workforce. Overall, the structure of the dataset suggests it is well-suited for predictive modeling and exploratory analysis.\n", "\n", "Now, let's look at anther method of finding summary statistics." ], For example, the average salary in the dataset is approximately \\$96,546, while the maximum salary exceeds $300,000. This large range suggests that salary values may be right-skewed and contain high-income outliers.\n", "\n", "We also see that years of eperience ranges from 0 to 19 years. This indicates that the dataset includes both entry-level and senior-level professionals. Additionally, employee satisfaction scores appear relatively high overall, with an average above 72 out of 100. This is good to notice." ], "metadata": { "id": "pJOlqolK2ro0" } }, { "cell_type": "markdown", "source": [ "Next, we want to examine the most common categories within each categorical variable. Understanding which values appear most frequently can reveal potential imbalance in the dataset and provide insight into how the workforce is represented across countries, industries, and job roles.\n" ], "metadata": { "id": "SQ-qjxQkGXxL" } }, { "cell_type": "code", "source": [ "for col in df.select_dtypes(include='object').columns:\n", " print(f\"Column \n" ], "application/vnd.google.colaboratory.intrinsic+json": { "type": "dataframe", "variable_name": "correlation_matrix" } }, "metadata": {} } ] }, { "cell_type": "markdown", "source": [ "From this correlation matrix, we can see that years of experience has a strong positive correlation with salary. This may suggest that workers with more experience generally earn higher salaries. Employee satisfaction also shows positive relationships with salary and job security score, while weekly work hours show a strong negative relationship with work-life balance score. These findings align with what we would expect, which suggests that many variables in the dataset contain useful predictive information." ], "metadata": { "id": "e_PbHTIzMr4m" } }, { "cell_type": "markdown", "source": [ "## **Exploratory Data Analysis**" ], "metadata": { "id": "F69iHV3HN-hw" } }, { "cell_type": "markdown", "source": [ "Using these summary statistics to gain an initial understanding of the workforce represented in the dataset and guide further study of the data, we begin to perform some exploratory data analysis. We will dive deeper into the data to uncover patterns.\n", "\n", "To begin, we want to know more about the distribution of the numerical columns. It is important to note that we are not intrested in all of them. For example, 'id' is going to be uniformly distributed and isn't a variable we are interested in anyways. In contrast, we may gain insights by looking at the distibution of variables like salary, bonus, etc." ], "metadata": { "id": "Jtx_ouwHQYRc" } }, { "cell_type": "code", "source": [ "import matplotlib.pyplot as plt\n", "\n", "df.hist(figsize=(15, 12), bins=30)\n", "plt.tight_layout()\n", "plt.show()" ], "metadata": { "colab": { "base_uri": "https://localhost:8080/", "height": 997 }, "id": "ZfUM4V4LQljJ", "outputId": "6bf49c60-d912-4a7e-d897-771d59b0e60f" }, "execution_count": 15, "outputs": [ { "output_type": "display_data", "data": { "text/plain": [ "
